title: carex vesicaria subsp. pulla
intro: Carex vesicaria subsp. pulla, also known as the hairy sedge, is a plant species in the sedge family (Cyperaceae) characterized by its distinctive hairy inflorescence. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Carex vesicaria subsp. pulla include its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use as a diuretic to help with urinary problems. Carex vesicaria subsp. pulla can be prepared as a tea or infusion for its medicinal properties, or it can be used in tincture or dried form in herbal remedies.
